title: -Amending the City Council’s Policy for the General Fund Reserves

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution amending the City Council’s Policy for the General Fund Reserves
and rescind Resolution No. 69,440-N.S.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There is no direct fiscal impact related to amending the policy. However, the policy is
intended to provide guidelines for ensuring an adequate reserve policy to assist the City
in mitigating current and future financial risks resulting from economic instability or
catastrophic loss.
sections.background:
A general fund reserve, or “rainy day fund,” ensures a government’s ability to maintain
vital services to the community during times of economic uncertainty and to help
mitigate the negative impacts of extraordinary risk such as earthquakes, fires and
floods. The City’s current policy includes two elements of the General Fund Reserves:
(1) A Stability Reserve is maintained to mitigate loss of service delivery and financial
risks associated with unexpected revenue shortfalls during a single fiscal year or
during a prolonged recessionary period. The purpose of the Stability Reserve is to
provide fiscal stability in response to unexpected downturns or revenue shortfalls,
and not to serve as a funding source for new programs or projects.
(2) A Catastrophic Reserve is maintained for the purpose of sustaining General Fund
operations in the case of a public emergency such as a natural disaster or other
catastrophic event. The Catastrophic Reserve will be used to respond to extreme,
onetime events, such as earthquakes, fires, floods, civil unrest, terrorist attacks,
public health emergencies and pandemics. The Catastrophic Reserve will not be
accessed to meet operational shortfalls or to fund new programs or projects.

Amending the Policy for the General Fund Reserves CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
On December 13, 2016, the City Council received a report to amend the General Fund
Reserve Policy as the previous policy set the target at 8 percent of General Fund
revenues, which was less than half the minimum 16.7 percent reserve level
recommended by the Government Finance Officers Association (GFOA). In order to
determine an appropriate target General Fund Reserve level for the City of Berkeley,
staff conducted a risk assessment. Based on the outcome of the risk assessment, it was
recommended that
sections.rationale:
Based on the risk assessment performed by GFOA, it was determined that General
Fund reserves should be a range between 20% to 30% of revenue. This should provide
the City with a reasonable level of reserves to manage the various risks (i.e., wildfires,
recessions and revenue volatility, earthquakes, high winds, tsunamis, pensions)
analyzed. It is important to note that the higher the level of reserves, the higher the
probability that the City will be able to maintain needed services during an emergency or
economic downturn.
sections.alternatives:
The Committee considered not changing the existing policy.
CONTACT PERSON
David White, Deputy City Manager, (510) 981-7000
Henry Oyekanmi, Director of Finance, (510) 981-7200

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AMENDING THE CITY COUNCIL'S POLICY FOR THE GENERAL FUND RESERVES
WHEREAS, the General Fund Reserves ensures the City's ability to maintain vital
services to the community during times of economic uncertainty; and
WHEREAS, the City is committed to achieving long-term fiscal stability as well as
mitigating the negative impacts of extraordinary risk such as earthquakes, fires, floods,
public health emergencies and pandemics; and
WHEREAS, specific portions of the reserves should be designated for catastrophic and
stabilization purposes and should be reported separately from the General Fund
Balance; and
WHEREAS, when using the General Fund Reserves the City should specify the
purpose for the use of reserve funds, the time frame in which the reserve funds can be
used, and how much will be used during that time frame; and
WHEREAS, the General Fund Reserves ensures the City's ability to maintain vital
services to the community during times of economic uncertainty; and
WHEREAS, the City is committed to achieving long-term fiscal stability as well as
mitigating the negative impacts of extraordinary risk such as earthquakes, fires, floods,
public health emergencies and pandemics; and
WHEREAS, Council adopted Resolution No. 69,440-N.S. on June 16, 2020 that
included a long-term goal of Reserves of a minimum of 30% of General Fund
Revenues, to be achieved within no more than 10 years (Fiscal Year 2027); and
